About: It is about athletic theater centered around mock combat.
It is about performers who are competitive wrestlers.
It is about the legitimate sport of wrestling.
It is about the history of professional wrestling in the United States.
It is about the transition of wrestling to theater.
It is about carnival culture's influence on professional wrestling.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with athletic theater.
It is associated with mock combat.
It is associated with competitive wrestlers.
It is associated with scripted victories.
It is associated with personae and gimmicks.
It is associated with heroic "faces" and villainous "heels".
It is associated with "kayfabe".
It is associated with promotions.
It is associated with a unique glossary of terms.
It is associated with national or international sports icons.
CausedBy: It is caused by the need for more entertaining action in wrestling.
It is caused by the lack of popularity of the legitimate sport of wrestling.
It is caused by the desire to please fans.
It is caused by the need to make wrestlers more entertaining.
It is caused by the need to keep match fixing a secret.
It is caused by the shift in audience tastes.
It is caused by the desire to minimize injury.
ConsistsOf: It consists of performers who are competitive wrestlers.
It consists of improvisation and choreography.
It consists of personae and gimmicks.
It consists of heroic "faces" and villainous "heels".
It consists of a unique glossary of terms.
CreatedFor: It was created for pleasing fans.
It was created for making wrestlers more entertaining.
It was created for drawing sustainable audiences.
CreatedTo: It was created to display more entertaining action.
